name: Sore Throat (Acute): Antimicrobial Prescribing
citation: NICE NG84 Clinical Guideline: Sore Throat (Acute): Antimicrobial Prescribing
rules: IF severe systemic infection or severe complications occur THEN Refer to hospital.
IF person is systemically very unwell OR has symptoms and signs of a more serious illness or condition OR has high risk of complications THEN Offer an immediate antibiotic prescription.
IF FeverPAIN score is 0 or 1 OR Centor score is 0, 1, or 2 THEN Do not offer an antibiotic.
IF FeverPAIN score is 2 or 3 THEN Consider no antibiotic or a back-up antibiotic prescription.
IF FeverPAIN score is 4 or 5 OR Centor score is 3 or 4 THEN Consider an immediate antibiotic or a back-up antibiotic prescription.

IF person requires self-care THEN Consider paracetamol for pain or fever or if preferred and suitable, ibuprofen; advise to drink adequate fluids; note that some evidence supports medicated lozenges to reduce pain in adults, but not non-medicated lozenges, mouthwashes, or local anaesthetic mouth spray on its own.

nodes.text: Severe systemic infection or severe complications
Person is systemically very unwell OR has symptoms and signs of a more serious illness or condition OR has high risk of complications
FeverPAIN score is 0 or 1 OR Centor score is 0, 1, or 2
Consider no antibiotic or a back-up antibiotic prescription
FeverPAIN score is 4 or 5 OR Centor score is 3 or 4
Consider an immediate antibiotic or a back-up antibiotic prescription

Advise that the antibiotic is not needed immediately; use prescription if no improvement in 3 to 5 days, or symptoms worsen, and seek medical help if symptoms worsen rapidly or significantly or if the person becomes very unwell
Advise to seek medical help if symptoms worsen rapidly or significantly or if the person becomes very unwell
Consider paracetamol for pain or fever or if preferred and suitable, ibuprofen
Note evidence supports medicated lozenges to reduce pain in adults, but not for non-medicated lozenges, mouthwashes, or local anaesthetic mouth sprays.
